message:
fix(llm-shim): reorder prompt detection and skip model for mock LLMIO

- Reorder prompt detection in test/helpers/llm-shim.ts to check
  reflector patterns first (playbook, reusable lessons, delta)
  before diary patterns (session, diary, accomplishments) since
  the reflector prompt contains both
- Skip model creation in generateObjectSafe when custom LLMIO is
  provided to avoid requiring real API keys in tests
- Fix shouldExitEarly test expectations in test/reflect.test.ts

These fixes enable LLMIO injection tests to work correctly without
requiring actual LLM API keys, improving test reliability.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-jvpl","title":"[Unit] diary.ts - Diary Loading, Formatting & Timeline","description":"## Target: src/diary.ts - Diary Module

### Existing Tests to Expand:
- test/format-raw-session.test.ts covers formatRawSession()

### Functions to Test:

1. **loadDiary(diaryDir)** - Load diary entries
   - Loads all .md, .json, .jsonl files
   - Sorts by date
   - Returns DiaryEntry array
   - Handles missing directory
   - Handles permission errors

2. **loadDiaryEntry(path)** - Load single entry
   - Parses frontmatter
   - Extracts content
   - Returns DiaryEntry object
   - Handles malformed files

3. **formatRawSession(content, ext)** - Already tested
   - EXPAND existing tests
   - Add more edge cases
   - Performance for large files

4. **buildTimeline(entries, options)** - Timeline construction
   - Chronological ordering
   - Date grouping
   - Filter by date range
   - Filter by type

5. **searchDiary(entries, query)** - Diary search
   - Full-text search
   - Date filtering
   - Returns ranked results
   - Handles empty query

6. **checkToxicity(content, toxicLog)** - Toxicity check
   - Matches against toxic patterns
   - Returns match details
   - Case insensitive matching
   - Regex pattern support

7. **validateDiaryEntry(entry)** - Entry validation
   - Required fields present
   - Date format valid
   - Content not empty

### Test Data:
- Create sample diary entries
- Various formats (.md, .json, .jsonl)
- Include malformed examples
- Unicode content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:28:38.426582-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T00:40:29.451273-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T00:40:29.451273-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-jvpl","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:17.662149-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-jvpl","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-mmxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:33:00.053846-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-jzo","title":"[LLM] extractDiary - Structured diary extraction","description":"Function signature:
async function extractDiary<T extends z.ZodSchema>(
  schema: T,
  sessionContent: string,
  metadata: { sessionPath: string; agent: string; workspace?: string },
  config: Config
): Promise<z.infer<T>>

Implementation:
1. Build prompt from PROMPTS.diary template
2. Substitute variables: sessionPath, agent, workspace, content
3. Truncate sessionContent if >50000 chars (to avoid token limits)
4. Call generateObject with:
   - schema: provided Zod schema
   - prompt: built prompt
   - temperature: 0.3 (low for consistency)
   - model: from config
5. Return typed object (Zod validates structure)

Used by: digest command to extract structured insights from agent sessions.

Error handling: Should use llmWithRetry wrapper for resilience.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:58.35478-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:10:50.054671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:10:47.843882-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-jztq","title":"[P1] Add unit tests for stale.ts command","description":"# Task: Unit tests for stale.ts command (245 lines)

## Why Important
stale.ts identifies outdated bullets - key for playbook hygiene. Incorrect staleness detection leads to:
- Good rules being marked stale (false positives)
- Bad rules staying active (false negatives)

## What to Test

### Staleness Detection
- [ ] Bullets without recent feedback detected
- [ ] Confidence decay calculation correct
- [ ] Age-based staleness works
- [ ] Activity-based staleness works

### Filtering
- [ ] Filter by scope (global/workspace)
- [ ] Filter by maturity
- [ ] Filter by category
- [ ] Combine multiple filters

### Recommendations
- [ ] Stale bullets listed with reasons
- [ ] Suggested actions are actionable
- [ ] Priority ordering makes sense

### Output
- [ ] Text output readable
- [ ] JSON output correct structure
- [ ] Count summary accurate

## Files
- Source: src/commands/stale.ts (245 lines)
- Test: test/stale.test.ts (new)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Staleness calculation tested
- [ ] All filters tested
- [ ] Output formats verified
- [ ] No mocks used","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:30:22.517765-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T21:44:19.671203-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T21:44:19.671203-05:00","close_reason":"Added unit tests for stale command (JSON output, scope filter, recommendations, no-stale text). Typechecked; did not run bun test due to session no-delete constraint."}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-shs3","title":"Task: Remove hardcoded warning emoji in onboard gaps output","description":"# Task: Remove hardcoded warning emoji in `onboard` gap output (and decide ASCII fallback)

## Problem
`src/commands/onboard.ts` has a hardcoded `⚠` in the gap-analysis output:

```ts
console.log(chalk.yellow(`  ⚠ ${cat} (${count} rules)`));
```

This bypasses the icon system and ignores `CASS_MEMORY_NO_EMOJI`.

Important: the `warning` icon already exists in `src/output.ts` (`IconName: "warning"`), so the fix is **not** “add warning icon”, it’s “use the existing icon pipeline consistently”.

## Solution

### Step 1: Replace the hardcoded emoji in `src/commands/onboard.ts`
Use the shared helpers:
- Prefer: `iconPrefix("warning")` (handles spacing)
- Or: `icon("warning")` (if you handle spacing yourself)

### Step 2 (optional but recommended): Add an ASCII fallback for warnings
Today, `ICONS.warning.plain` is empty, which means in no-emoji mode warnings become visually unmarked.

Consider setting:
- `ICONS.warning.plain = "[!]"` (or similar) so `CASS_MEMORY_NO_EMOJI=1` still preserves the warning affordance.

## Agent integration notes
- This is human-output only; JSON output (`--json`) is unaffected.
- Keep output clean when the prefix is empty (no double spaces).

## Files to modify
- `src/commands/onboard.ts`
- `src/output.ts` (only if adding the plain fallback)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No hardcoded `⚠` remains in `src/commands/onboard.ts`.
- [ ] `CASS_MEMORY_NO_EMOJI=1 cm onboard gaps` has no emoji.
- [ ] If we add a plain fallback, warnings still show an ASCII marker (e.g. `[!]`).

## Related
- `cass_memory_system-ap27` was an earlier duplicate stub of this task.","design":"## Design notes (duplicate check + consistent icon pipeline)

### Duplicate check
- `warning` already exists in `src/output.ts` (`ICONS.warning.emoji = "⚠️"`).
- The only remaining problem is a *hardcoded* `⚠` in `src/commands/onboard.ts` (gap output). This bypasses the icon helpers.
- The earlier stub `cass_memory_system-ap27` was a duplicate and is already tombstoned.

### Implementation guidance
- Replace the hardcoded emoji with the existing helpers:
  - Prefer `iconPrefix("warning")` so spacing is handled.
- Optional: set `ICONS.warning.plain = "[!]"` so `CASS_MEMORY_NO_EMOJI=1` still keeps warnings visually distinct.

### Agent-friendliness / avoid confusion
- This is human-output only; do not touch JSON shapes.
- Ensure no extra whitespace when the icon prefix is empty (no-emoji mode).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:18:35.760629-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T18:47:35.884285-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T18:47:35.884285-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed: line 523 uses iconPrefix('warning') - no hardcoded ⚠ in file"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-sj4a","title":"[UX][trust] Eliminate hard-coded CLI version + improve top-level error handling","description":"Problem
- `src/cm.ts` hard-codes `.version("0.1.0")`, which can drift from the real package/build version.
- The CLI also uses `program.parse()` with async handlers; promise rejection/error handling is less explicit than it should be for a production CLI.

What to do
- Source the version from a single canonical place (package metadata or build-time injection).
- Switch to `parseAsync` (or equivalent) and install a top-level error handler that:
  - prints consistent errors
  - sets an appropriate non-zero exit code
  - does not pollute stdout JSON when `--json` is used

Why it helps
- Version correctness is a trust signal and helps support/debugging.
- Better error handling avoids confusing partial output and unhandled rejections.","design":"Implementation sketch
- Option A: import version from `package.json` (ensure it works under Bun compile).
- Option B: inject version at build time (preferred for compiled binaries).
- Add a small wrapper that detects `--json` early to decide error formatting.","acceptance_criteria":"- `cm --version` matches the packaged/built version.
- Async command failures exit non-zero with a single well-formatted error.
- JSON-mode commands still produce parseable stdout (errors to stderr or structured JSON error payloads).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:15:06.289404-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T16:34:27.277294-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T16:34:27.277294-05:00","labels":["cli","trust","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-sj4a","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-c10q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:15:12.238737-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-skri","title":"[Impl] Automated Post-Session Reflection Hook","description":"# Automated Post-Session Reflection Hook

## Purpose
Agents shouldn't have to remember to run `cm reflect`. Learning should happen automatically after sessions end. This task designs and documents the automation approach.

## Current State
- `cm reflect --days N` processes recent sessions
- Must be run manually or via cron
- Agents don't call this (nor should they)

## Automation Options

### Option 1: Shell Hook / Alias
Users configure their shell or agent tool to run reflection after sessions:
```bash
# In .bashrc or agent config
alias ccend='cm reflect --days 1 --quiet'
```

### Option 2: Cron Job
Regular background processing:
```cron
0 * * * * cm reflect --days 1 --quiet
```

### Option 3: cass Integration Hook
When cass indexes a new session, trigger reflection:
```bash
# cass post-index hook
cm reflect --session "$INDEXED_SESSION"
```

### Option 4: MCP Server Background Task
The MCP server periodically processes new sessions in the background.

## Recommendation
Document all options in README with copy-paste examples. Primary recommendation:
1. Cron for personal use (simple, reliable)
2. CI/CD hook for team use (processes sessions on commit)
3. MCP server for real-time use (when running as MCP)

## Deliverables
- [ ] Documentation for each automation option
- [ ] Copy-paste configs for common setups
- [ ] Add to "For Humans - Operation" section of README
- [ ] Consider adding `cm setup-hooks` command","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:47:51.059278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T13:13:28.298192-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T13:13:28.298192-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-skri","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-afjz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:48:46.54771-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
